description: The bucket's raw_html_path and track_b_row for 'Nymeria' point to the historical Princess Nymeria of the Rhoynar (sources/wiki/_raw/Nymeria.json), not Arya's direwolf. The correct page is sources/wiki/_raw/Nymeria_(direwolf).json. I built the node from the direwolf page and ignored the track_b_row data (which contained titles like Princess of Dorne, allegiances to Rhoynar/House Martell, etc.). The triage script should map this bucket entry to 'Nymeria_(direwolf)' instead of 'Nymeria'. Node emitted as nymeria-direwolf.node.md using wiki infobox data from the correct page.

The Dragonkeepers are classified as organization.house in the bucket input, but they are an order of guards (dragon protectors), not a noble house. Reclassified to organization.faction in the emitted node. Confirm this is correct or if a different type is preferred.

The track_b_row.first_available field for Sansa Stark reports ASOS chapter 1 (Prologue), but her cite_refs include AGOT chapters 4, 6, 7, 14, 15, 16, etc. Sansa clearly first appears in AGOT, not ASOS. This appears to be a parser bug in the first_available calculation — the parser may be picking up the wrong minimum cite_ref. The node was written with the parser's value verbatim per the agent rules, but the value is almost certainly wrong.

Colemon first_available from cite_ref parser says AFFC Ch.10, but the same page cite_refs show agot-25, 27, 34, 40. He clearly appears from AGOT onward. The parser first_available value appears wrong — set to "always available" in node and flagging here.

Parser first_available was ACOK 11, but cite_refs include agot:[0,28,29,57,63,71]; pass1_mentions in agot-prologue show earlier appearances. Node set to "always available". Please verify correct first_available.

The track_b_row SPOUSE_OF edge for Tommard Heddle targets "Ambrose Butterwell" but the wiki infobox actually says "Eldest daughter of Ambrose Butterwell" (an unnamed character). The parser extracted the linked page name (Ambrose) rather than the relationship description. The SPOUSE_OF edge in the emitted node targets the unnamed daughter, not Ambrose. This is a systematic parser issue: when the spouse field links to a different entity than the actual spouse, the parser captures the link target.

The track_b_row types Caltrops as event.war, but the wiki page describes a conspiracy of 13 named nobles during the Dance of the Dragons who plotted to kill the Two Betrayers (Hugh Hammer and Ulf White). The wiki categories include "Organizations". Reclassified to organization.faction in the emitted node. This is a group of conspirators, not a war or battle.

evidence: Jeor gives the blade to Jon, who notes the appropriateness of a bastard wielding a bastard sword.
description: Longclaw's prose describes two gifting events: (1) Jeor Mormont gives Longclaw to his son Jorah when Jeor joins the Night's Watch; (2) Jeor then gives the blade to Jon Snow after Jon saves his life. These are owner-transfer events with named giver and recipient that are narratively significant — the Longclaw gifting to Jon Snow is one of the most important object-transfer moments in AGOT. No current edge type captures this. OWNED_BY / WIELDS are wrong direction from an artifact node, and neither captures the transfer act. Proposed new type: GIFTED_TO (Giver → Recipient, with artifact as subject context). Example sentences: (1) 'Lord Jeor Mormont gave the blade to his son, Jorah Mormont, when Jeor joined the Night's Watch.' (2) 'Jeor gives the blade to Jon, who notes the appropriateness of a bastard wielding a bastard sword.' (3) 'His aunt, Lady Maege Mormont, returned the sword to her brother Jeor.' The third example is a RETURNED variant — could be covered by the same type with a qualifier. Without this type, all gifting/transfer moments are silently dropped as no-fitting-edge-type rejects. Affects: longclaw→jon-snow (gifting), longclaw→jorah-mormont (gifting), and likely other artifact transfer edges across the graph (Dawn passed to Ashara/unknown, Ice's history, Needle given to Arya by Jon).

evidence: There are ripples in the dark steel indicating it is of Valyrian steel.
description: Longclaw's prose repeatedly states that the blade is made of Valyrian steel — a material composition relationship. The current vocabulary has no MADE_OF, COMPOSED_OF, or FORGED_FROM edge type. FORGED_BY covers the creator/smith relationship, not the material. For Valyrian steel swords specifically, this relationship is highly queryable: 'show me all Valyrian steel weapons' requires artifact→material edges. Proposed new type: MADE_OF (Artifact → Material). Example sentences: (1) 'There are ripples in the dark steel indicating it is of Valyrian steel.' (2) 'The blade itself was Valyrian steel, old and light and deadly sharp.' (3) 'A man who bears Valyrian steel should use it for more than scratching his arse.' This gap affects all Valyrian steel weapons (Ice, Dawn, Heartsbane, Oathkeeper, Widow's Wail, Needle, etc.) and other artifact-to-material relationships across the graph. Without it, material composition is silently lost.

Need a DEFEATED_BY edge type (or symmetric DEFEATS with explicit direction) for cases where the source node is the loser of a combat and the target is the victor. Current vocab has DEFEATS but no DEFEATED_BY. Example: Edmund Ambrose (source) was defeated by Brienne of Tarth (target) at the melee at Bitterbridge. The candidate is source=edmund-ambrose, target=brienne-tarth, but DEFEATS would go victor→loser. Should we add DEFEATED_BY, or should the edge always be emitted in victor→loser direction (i.e., from the victor's node file)?

No edge type covers a character adopting a location or structure as a heraldic sigil/symbol. In the Baelish hedge-knight article, he 'took the head of the Titan of Braavos as his sigil when he was knighted.' The Titan of Braavos is typed place.location in the graph. No existing type handles character→location with meaning 'took as heraldic motif.' OWNS is too material, WIELDS is for artifacts only, and there is no HERALDIC_SYMBOL_OF or USES_AS_SIGIL type. Should we add a USES_AS_SIGIL edge type (character→object.artifact or place.location) or should these be rejected as non-relational?

evidence: Melisandre places a [glamor] over him, making him appear to be the wildling Rattleshirt.
description: Melisandre creates and employs shadow children (concept.magic) and glamors (concept.magic), and holds nightfires (concept.magic) as priestly practice. These are clear relational edges — she is the practitioner/creator/employer of these magical techniques — but no vocabulary type covers 'character creates or employs a magical construct or technique.' WIELDS requires object.artifact; MEMBER_OF requires organization.*; OWNS is semantically wrong for an intangible magical act. Proposed new type: PRACTICES (character → concept.magic) for ongoing ritual/magical practice, OR EMPLOYS for active magical use. Example sentences from Melisandre's node: (1) 'Melisandre places a glamor over him, making him appear to be the wildling Rattleshirt.' (2) 'She creates a shadow which kills Renly the morning he is to march to battle against Stannis.' (3) 'Melisandre holds her nightfires at Castle Black.' This gap affects all red priests (Thoros of Myr practicing resurrection, Moqorro), wargs (practicing warging separately from WARGS_INTO which is active occupation), and other magic-users across the graph.

evidence: and Melisandre [performs the ceremony].
description: Melisandre officiates/performs the wedding ceremony at the Wedding of Sigorn and Alys Karstark (event.battle in the node graph). The relationship is 'character officiates at event' but no vocabulary type covers this. FIGHTS_IN is semantically wrong (it is a wedding not a battle); ADVISES is too weak; CAUSES overstates her agency. Proposed new type: OFFICIATES (character → event) for ceremony performance. Example sentences: (1) 'Melisandre performs the ceremony.' (2) 'The High Septon officiated at the royal wedding.' (3) 'Septon Chayle conducted the ceremony in the sept.' This gap also affects High Septons and other religious officials who perform ceremonies across the graph.

evidence: Lambert attends the [wedding of Sigorn and Alys Karstark](wiki:Wedding_of_Sigorn_and_Alys_Karstark).
description: Several minor characters in this bucket (and likely elsewhere) attend named social events such as weddings and feasts. The candidate Lambert Whitewater attends the Wedding of Sigorn and Alys Karstark establishes a real relationship between a character and an event node, but no edge type covers event-attendance for social/ceremonial events. LOCATED_AT is blocked because its target type contract requires place.location, not event.*. FIGHTS_IN is combat-only. CONTEMPORARY_WITH is for entities co-existing in time, not active attendance. Proposed new edge type: ATTENDS (character -> event.*) covering presence at social/ceremonial events (weddings, feasts, councils, tournaments as spectator). Examples: (1) Lambert Whitewater attends the Wedding of Sigorn and Alys Karstark. (2) Various lords attend a tourney without competing (spectator vs FIGHTS_IN). (3) Characters present at a feast or council who are not fighting. Without this type, all such edges must be rejected as just-mentions, silently losing the attendance relationship.

evidence: Henly Ashford purchases a horse (Sweetfoot) from Duncan the Tall at the tourney at Ashford Meadow.
description: Clear commercial transaction between characters — no type in current 121-type vocab covers commerce/purchase between persons. Emitted OWNS→sweetfoot for the resulting ownership. The purchaser→seller relationship has no home. Recommend PURCHASED_FROM(buyer→seller) or similar.

evidence: Maelys was a descendant of the male line of Daemon I Blackfyre
description: Several Blackfyre characters are described as descendants of Daemon I Blackfyre or other ancestors. The natural edge direction ANCESTOR_OF(ancestor→descendant) exists, but the candidate shape presents source=descendant→target=ancestor, requiring DESCENDED_FROM(descendant→ancestor) which is not in vocabulary. Without it, all descendant→ancestor relationships must be rejected or the edge must be reversed (but we cannot emit edges where source≠candidate source). Proposed: DESCENDED_FROM (character → character) for explicit genealogical descent. Example: Maelys I Blackfyre DESCENDED_FROM Daemon I Blackfyre.

evidence: Baelor attended the kingsmoot on Old Wyk
description: Baelor Blacktyde attended the kingsmoot on Old Wyk where Euron Greyjoy was crowned. ATTENDS has already been proposed (q-2026-05-14-characters-house-baratheon-of-dragonstone-014) for social/ceremonial events; this is a second instance confirming the gap is systematic. Political assemblies (kingsmoot, great councils, small council meetings) share the same shape: character is physically present at an event node without fighting. FIGHTS_IN is wrong; LOCATED_AT requires place.location target (event.* blocked). Reinforcing prior proposal: ATTENDS (character → event.*) is needed.

evidence: title and lands are given to Bennarion's uncle, Germund Botley
description: Kinship vocabulary lacks uncle/nephew (and aunt/niece) types. PARENT_OF covers one generation; SIBLING_OF covers same generation; ANCESTOR_OF covers more than one generation. Uncle/nephew is one generation removed laterally — not covered by any existing type. Encountered repeatedly: Bennarion, Harlon, Vickon, Harren, Tristifer, Maron, Symond Botley (all Sawane's children) each have Germund Botley (Sawane's brother) as their uncle. All candidate edges rejected with vocab-gap. Proposed: NEPHEW_OF (nephew→uncle) or equivalently UNCLE_OF (uncle→nephew), symmetric or asymmetric.

evidence: Lord Germund is listed as one of Euron's champions at the kingsmoot
description: Kingsmoot attendance reinforces prior gap (q-2026-05-14-batch-0004-vocab-gap-002 for ATTENDS). Germund Botley listed as one of Euron's champions at the kingsmoot — political assembly attendance, not a battle. FIGHTS_IN is semantically wrong (no combat); LOCATED_AT blocked for event.* targets; MEMBER_OF targets organizations not events. Third instance of this pattern confirming systematic gap. Reinforcing: ATTENDS (character → event.*) needed for political assemblies, great councils, kingmoots, etc.

evidence: Alysanne or one of her sisters was raped by Ser Gregor Clegane during the burning of Stone Hedge
description: No edge type exists for sexual violence victimization. Five Bracken sisters (Alysanne, Barbara, Bess, Catelyn, Jayne) each have wiki prose stating they were raped by Gregor Clegane during the Burning of Stone Hedge. This is a documented atrocity graphically important for understanding Jonos Bracken motivations. FIGHTS_AGAINST and RESENTS are indirect (Jonos vs Gregor); no direct victimization type exists for the sisters. Proposed: VICTIMIZED_BY (character -> character, covers assault/rape/torture by target against source). Distinct from KILLED_BY, covers non-lethal atrocities.

evidence: ramsay-snow→reek: Ramsay employed Reek as a serving man; SERVES(reek→ramsay) is inverse only
royce-blackwood: Lord Blackwood attempted to betroth his son to Princess Daella Targaryen; no BETROTHED_TO type
barba-bolton: Barba petitioned Aegon III to send food to the North if he didn't choose her as queen
He is the cousin of Ser Bennard Brune, the Knight of Brownhollow
description: Should COUSIN_OF be added to the locked edge vocabulary? Attested here for eustace-brune→bennard-brune. Currently no first-cousin kinship type exists; SIBLING_OF covers siblings only.

evidence: Lord Buckler hosts Prince Oberyn Martell and his entourage
description: Should HOST_OF (host→guest, inverse of GUEST_OF) be added? ralph-buckler hosts oberyn-martell. GUEST_OF (guest→host) exists but no inverse. Would HOST_OF be a redundant symmetric equivalent, or a distinct directed type?

Cosgrove (a steward at Whitewalls) was bribed by Ser Uthor Underleaf to give him favorable tournament matches at the wedding tourney. There is no edge type for 'bribed by' / 'bribes'. The relationship is: Uthor Underleaf → bribes → Cosgrove, with the result being favorable tournament scheduling. Proposed new type: BRIBES (briber → bribee) or BRIBED_BY (bribee → briber). This is a distinct action from CONSPIRES_WITH (no shared goal) or COMMANDS (no authority relationship). Without it, this class of corrupt-exchange relationships is silently dropped.

Lord Butterwell (grandfather of Ambrose) had Whitewalls built. There is no edge type for 'built' / 'founded' / 'constructed'. The relationship is: lord-butterwell-grandfather-of-ambrose → built → whitewalls. LOCATED_AT is wrong (it's not where he lives permanently), HOSTS is wrong (that's for events). Proposed new types: BUILT or FOUNDED (builder/founder → location/structure). This class of 'constructed this place' relationships is architecturally significant (builders of castles, cities, walls) and currently has no vocabulary support.

evidence: Mylenda is rumored to have had an affair with Walder Frey, the son of Ryman Frey
description: Should LOVERS_WITH or LIAISED_WITH be added for rumored/attested affairs? Current vocab has no type for romantic liaisons that aren't formal marriages. ALLIED_WITH is too political; RELATED_TO is wrong (no kinship). This pattern recurs for bastard-producing affairs and noble scandal relationships.

evidence: Cleyton Caswell was a suitor who sought to court Rohanne Webber
description: Should COURTED (suitor→object-of-courtship) be added? Rohanne Webber famously had many suitors. COURTED is distinct from BETROTHED_TO (no formal betrothal) and MARRIED (no marriage). The suitor relationship is plot-relevant in the Dunk & Egg stories. Alternatively, would ALLIED_WITH do as a fallback given the social pursuit context?

evidence: Arthor was in the manse of his kinsman, Lord Bartimos Celtigar
description: Should KINSMAN_OF be added for unspecified kinship (cousin, uncle, etc.) where the exact degree is unknown? Currently emitting RELATED_TO as fallback, but that's very generic. KINSMAN_OF would be more semantically specific. Alternatively, should we infer the specific degree from other evidence, or just leave RELATED_TO as the catch-all?

evidence: Jon Connington raised and protected Young Griff (Aegon Targaryen) for approximately 12 years in exile across Essos
description: Should GUARDIAN_OF (or RAISES) be added for long-term guardian/ward relationships? Jon Connington raised Aegon Targaryen for ~12 years in exile — acting as guardian, protector, and primary caretaker. This is distinct from: TUTORS (skill instruction), SERVES (fealty to a liege), ADMIRES (emotional regard), COMMANDS (military hierarchy). The guardian→ward relationship is plot-critical for understanding Jon's motivations and his greyscale infection. Pattern may recur for other ward-guardian pairs (e.g., Ned Stark and Theon Greyjoy, Tywin Lannister and Oberyn Martell's daughters).

evidence: Though he is introduced as Ser Robert Strong, many in the Red Keep have guessed the identity of the silent giant — an 8-foot warrior reanimated by Qyburn after Gregor Clegane was fatally poisoned by Oberyn Martell's spear
evidence_section: ## Narrative Arc / ### A Dance with Dragons
description: Should gregor-clegane and robert-strong be merged as SAME_AS nodes? Robert Strong is Gregor Clegane reanimated/zombified by Qyburn — same physical body, introduced under a cover identity in ADWD. The wiki maintains separate pages for the two, but they are the same entity. Recommend SAME_AS(gregor-clegane, robert-strong) with gregor-clegane as the canonical node.

evidence: Clarence Charlton participates in the tourney at Whitewalls. Sandor Clegane unhorsed Renly Baratheon at the Hand's tourney. Gregor Clegane defeated Balon Swann at the Hand's tourney.
description: Should COMPETES_IN be added for tournament participation? Current vocab has FIGHTS_IN for battles/wars only — tournament jousts/melee participation have no type. Pattern appears across many characters (clarence-charlton, sandor-clegane, gregor-clegane, and likely 50+ others). COMPETES_IN(character → tournament_event) would capture structured tournament participation distinct from combat.

evidence: attended the golden wedding
description: No edge type for a person attending a specific event (wedding, ceremony, feast) as a guest or participant — not fighting in it (FIGHTS_IN), not organizing it (COMMANDS), not performing at it. Example: qarl-corbray attended the 'golden-wedding' event. Rejected as just_mention for now.

evidence: his uncle Corwyn Corbray
description: No kinship edge type for uncle/nephew relationship. Vocabulary has PARENT_OF, CHILD_OF, SIBLING_OF, COUSIN_OF, GRANDPARENT_OF but not UNCLE_OF or NEPHEW_OF (one generation removed via sibling). Example: corwyn-corbray is uncle of quenton-corbray. Rejected as just_mention; using CONTEMPORARY_WITH as fallback is wrong.

evidence: slain by Orphan-Maker
description: No edge type for a person being killed by a specific named artifact in combat (not execution). EXECUTED_WITH exists for judicial execution, but combat death by a specific weapon (e.g., 'slain by Orphan-Maker') has no type. KILLED_BY takes a person agent, not an artifact. Example: owen-costayne killed by jon-roxton using orphan-maker. The artifact-as-agent-of-death has no slot. Rejected orphan-maker candidate as just_mention.

evidence: Amidst the fighting, Gerold attempts to kill Myrcella. Her horse rears, however, and Darkstar succeeds only in slicing off her ear and scarring her face.
evidence_section: ## Narrative Arc / ### A Feast for Crows
description: Gerold Dayne physically assaults Myrcella Baratheon, severing her ear and scarring her face. This is not a KILLS (she survived), not a DUELS (one-sided attack, not combat between peers), not a POISONS, and not an EXECUTES. The prose establishes a clear relational act of violence — attacker→victim — that has no fitting canonical edge type. Suggest ATTACKS (attacker → victim, non-lethal assault, possibly covers attempted-kills and woundings). Example sentences: (1) 'Gerold attempts to kill Myrcella. Her horse rears, and Darkstar succeeds only in slicing off her ear.' (2) Similar cases: Jaime loses his hand to Vargo Hoat (maiming). (3) The Tickler torturing captives. All these represent violence-without-death that currently has no typed edge.

evidence: Edric Dayne and Jon Snow were both nursed by Wylla — making them milk brothers.
description: MILK_BROTHER_OF (or FOSTER_SIBLING_OF): two characters nursed by the same wet-nurse. No canonical equivalent. Mapped to CONTEMPORARY_WITH as fallback but this loses kinship semantics. Appears for edric-dayne → jon-snow. Should a kinship edge be added?

evidence: Wylla is the wet nurse who nursed both Edric Dayne and (reportedly) Jon Snow.
description: NURSED_BY (nursling → wet_nurse) or WET_NURSE_OF (nurse → nursling): no canonical edge for wet-nursing relationship. Mapped to SERVES (wylla SERVES edric-dayne) which is backwards and wrong semantically — Wylla is rendering a service but SERVES implies subordination. A dedicated NURSED_BY or WET_NURSE_OF edge type would be more accurate. Also covers edric-dayne → wylla direction.

evidence: Edric Dayne is the son of Lord Dayne (his father).
description: CHILD_OF reverse direction: PARENT_OF(parent → child) exists but no CHILD_OF(child → parent). When source is the child, I mapped to CONTEMPORARY_WITH as fallback. Either add CHILD_OF as the reverse, or invert the edge direction to PARENT_OF(lord-dayne → edric) — but the candidate file lists edric as source_slug. Affects: edric-dayne → lord-dayne-father-of-edric, simon-dondarrion → lord-dondarrion-father-of-simon.

evidence: Thoros of Myr resurrected Beric Dondarrion multiple times via the Lord of Light.
description: RESURRECTED_BY (resurrected → resurrector): RESURRECTS(resurrector → resurrected) exists but the reverse direction has no canonical type. When beric-dondarrion is the source_slug, there is no correct edge. Mapped to CONTEMPORARY_WITH as fallback. Should RESURRECTED_BY be added as the reverse of RESURRECTS?

evidence: Beric Dondarrion knights Gendry in a ceremony at the hollow hill.
description: KNIGHTS / BESTOWS_KNIGHTHOOD_ON (knight → recipient): no canonical edge for the act of knighting someone. Mapped to COMMANDS(beric → gendry) as fallback, which is inaccurate — Beric is not commanding Gendry, he is granting him knighthood. COMMANDS implies an ongoing authority relationship. Should a dedicated knighting edge be added?

evidence: Lord Dondarrion hosted King Jaehaerys I Targaryen and Queen Alysanne at Blackhaven.
description: HOSTED_BY / HOST_OF: GUEST_OF(guest → host) exists but HOST_OF(host → guest) does not. When a lord hosts royalty, the lord is the source_slug but there is no canonical edge for 'A hosted B at their castle.' Mapped to CONTEMPORARY_WITH. Also: should GUEST_OF be used instead (inverting direction from lord → king to king → lord)?

evidence: Simon Dondarrion crowned Daenerys Targaryen (daughter of Jaehaerys I) Queen of Love and Beauty at a tourney.
description: Crowning someone Queen of Love and Beauty at a tournament is a specific meaningful relationship (romantic honor / political statement) with no canonical edge type. Mapped to ATTENDS (simon → daenerys) which doesn't capture the relational act. Should a specific edge type exist, or should CROWNS_QUEEN_OF_LOVE_AND_BEAUTY be canonicalized?

evidence: "Brave Danny Flint" is a sad and pretty song written about her.
description: Need an edge type for: character is the subject/protagonist of an in-world text (song, book, poem). Current vocab has WRITTEN_BY (Text→Author) and SUBJECT_OF_PROPHECY (Person→Prophecy) but nothing for Person→in-world-text when that text is about them. Candidate examples: (1) danny-flint → brave-danny-flint (Brave Danny Flint is a sad and pretty song written about her); (2) rains-of-castamere song was written about the destruction of House Reyne — the House could have a DEPICTED_IN edge to the song; (3) the song The Bear and the Maiden Fair — characters depicted in songs. Proposed type: DEPICTED_IN (Person/House → object.text); direction Person→Text. Alternative: expand SUBJECT_OF_PROPHECY to SUBJECT_OF covering any in-world text, not just prophecies.

evidence: Addam and his squire attended the marriage of his cousin to Lord Ambrose Butterwell.
description: Multiple Frey characters have cousin relationships that are explicitly named in prose but have no canonical edge type. COUSIN_OF would express collateral kinship beyond SIBLING_OF. Example 1: Addam Frey attends the marriage of his cousin Lady Butterwell. Example 2: Arwood Frey has a niece Amerei Frey. Example 3: Cleos Frey is described as Jaime Lannister's cousin. The kinship vocabulary has UNCLE_OF/NEPHEW_OF but no lateral-cousin type. Proposed: COUSIN_OF (symmetric, character → character) for first-cousin and second-cousin relationships explicitly named in prose.

evidence: the infant Harrold became the heir presumptive of his great-uncle Jon Arryn, Lord of the Eyrie.
description: UNCLE_OF is defined as a one-generation kinship shortcut (parent's sibling). ASOIAF prose frequently uses 'great-uncle' (grandparent's sibling) and 'great-nephew' (grandchild's sibling's child) for characters two generations removed — a common pattern in the Arryn, Harlaw, and likely other noble families. The existing UNCLE_OF type explicitly scopes to 'parent's sibling' and its guidance says to use it 'when prose explicitly says his uncle X / her aunt Y' — not 'great-uncle'. Three example sentences: (1) 'the infant Harrold became the heir presumptive of his great-uncle Jon Arryn, Lord of the Eyrie' (Harrold Hardyng, Origins). (2) 'Asha believes that Sigfryd would be opposed for he intends to follow his great nephew as Lord of the Ten Towers' — Sigfryd Harlaw's great-nephew is Rodrik Harlaw (Sigfryd Harlaw, AFFC). (3) The relationship between Jasper Arryn's children and their great-nieces/nephews in the Arryn family tree. Proposed type: GREAT_UNCLE_OF (great-uncle/aunt → great-nephew/niece; reverse GREAT_NEPHEW_OF). Symmetry: directed. Distinct from ANCESTOR_OF (which implies generational depth but applies to same-era contemporaries awkwardly) and UNCLE_OF (one hop, not two).

evidence: Hotho offers his support to Victarion Greyjoy and offers the Lord Captain his daughter for his queen.
description: The wiki prose for Hotho Harlaws daughter describes her being offered as a bride to Victarion Greyjoy (and separately to Rodrik Harlaw), but no betrothal was formally established in either case. BETROTHED_TO requires actual commitment; MARRIES_OFF goes on the arrangers node (Hotho), not the offered persons node; no type captures being offered as a bride without acceptance. Suggested type: PROPOSED_AS_BRIDE (offered person to potential spouse) or OFFERED_AS_BRIDE. Examples: (1) Hotho offers the Lord Captain his daughter for his queen -- hotho-harlaws-daughter to victarion-greyjoy (AFFC); (2) Hotho goes to Ten Towers hoping to persuade Rodrik to marry his daughter -- hotho-harlaws-daughter to rodrik-harlaw (AFFC); (3) Cersei was presented to Robb Stark as a potential bride -- per classifier failure-pattern docs this same gap was noted. Frequency: this pattern (daughter offered as political marriage chip, rejected or unmaterialized) recurs across many Westerosi noble families.

evidence: Gevin is listed among the crew of *Sea Bitch*.
description: The ASOS appendix lists named characters as crew members of specific ships. Sea Bitch is object.artifact (a ship). No edge type captures person-as-crew-member-of-ship. MEMBER_OF requires organization.faction target; Sea Bitch is object.artifact. SERVES and OWNS do not apply. Suggested type: CREW_OF (character to ship artifact). Examples: (1) Gevin is listed among the crew of Sea Bitch -- gevin-harlaw to sea-bitch (ASOS appendix); (2) many named ironborn are listed as crew of Iron Victory, Silence, Black Wind, etc. in ASOS appendix; (3) Asha Greyjoy commands the Black Wind and her crew members would need CREW_OF black-wind edges. This pattern will recur for all ship-crew listings across the ASOS appendix and any later appendices.

evidence: One of the towers of the castle was renamed the Widow's Tower in her honor.
description: ASOIAF prose regularly describes places named in honor of specific people. No current edge type captures the named-in-honor-of relationship. Proposed NAMED_FOR (character → named place, direction: honouree → named location). Example sentences: (1) 'One of the towers of the castle was renamed the Widow's Tower in her honor' (gwynesse-harlaw, ## Origins, referring to Widow's Tower at Ten Towers); (2) 'Maegor's Holdfast' (named after Maegor I, multiple nodes); (3) 'Lord Harroway's Town' (named after House Harroway, alys-harroway node). This pattern recurs across ASOIAF geography — keeps, towers, towns, and streets named for individuals. Without NAMED_FOR the relationship is a reject_just_mention, losing the commemorative link.

evidence: every family member of hers present in King's Landing was executed. Horas was thrown onto the spikes of the dry moat around Maegor's Holdfast.
description: Proposed new type: RELATED_TO (generic kinship shortcut for when only 'family member' is stated without specifying the exact relationship). Pattern recurs across many house wiki pages where the kinship type (sibling, parent, uncle, cousin) is not named. Three example sentences from this batch: (1) 'every family member of hers present in King's Landing was executed. Horas was thrown onto the spikes of the dry moat around Maegor's Holdfast.' (horas-harroway re Alys Harroway) (2) 'Lord Harroway's children, brothers, and nephews at King's Landing were executed' (lucas-harroway page — generic family grouped together) (3) Pattern repeats across many minor house pages where characters are described as 'a Harroway' or 'of House Frey' with no specific kinship stated. The existing kinship types (SIBLING_OF, PARENT_OF, COUSIN_OF, UNCLE_OF, NEPHEW_OF, MILK_BROTHER_OF) all require specific relationship knowledge; a generic RELATED_TO would capture cases where the prose only confirms shared family membership without specifying the type.

evidence: Denys hired the Faceless Men to assassinate a rival in King's Landing.
description: Proposed new type: CONTRACTED_WITH (person contracts an organization for a paid service, typically assassination or mercenary work). Distinct from ALLIES_WITH (mutual alliance), SERVES (employment), and MEMBER_OF (membership). The hiring of the Faceless Men is a recurring ASOIAF narrative pattern where a client pays for a contract killing. Three example sentences: (1) 'Denys hired the Faceless Men to assassinate a rival in King's Landing' (denys-harte page) (2) The broader pattern includes: 'paid [Faceless Men] a coin and spoke a name' (canonical Faceless Men hiring ritual across ASOIAF) (3) Characters hiring the Faceless Men recurs in Dany's storyline, Arya, and various nobles — a frequent enough pattern to warrant a typed edge. Without CONTRACTED_WITH, the only options are to misuse MANIPULATES (wrong — Faceless Men know they're being hired) or SEEKS (wrong direction/semantics) or reject silently. Current action: rejected as no-fitting-type-vocab-gap-filed.

evidence: Alerie echoes her good-mother, Lady Olenna Tyrell, that King Tommen I Baratheon should share a bed with Margaery
description: The good-mother (mother-in-law) and good-daughter (daughter-in-law) kinship relationship is narratively significant in ASOIAF but has no canonical edge type. Alerie Hightower married Mace Tyrell, making Olenna Tyrell her good-mother (Westerosi term for mother-in-law). Three examples: (1) "Alerie echoes her good-mother, Lady Olenna Tyrell, that King Tommen I Baratheon should share a bed with Margaery" (AFFC — Alerie defers to Olenna on Tommen/Margaery sleeping arrangements); (2) "Alerie: Mother. Olenna: Hush, Alerie, don't take that tone with me. And don't call me Mother. If I'd given birth to you, I'm sure I'd remember." (ASOS — dialogue confirms good-mother/good-daughter dynamic); (3) "Lady Alerie attends the funeral of Lord Tywin Lannister with her family" (AFFC — Alerie operates as extended Tyrell family alongside Olenna). Proposed types: DAUGHTER_IN_LAW_OF (directed: daughter-in-law → mother-in-law) and MOTHER_IN_LAW_OF (directed: mother-in-law → daughter-in-law). These differ from SPOUSE_OF (direct marriage) and PARENT_OF (blood/adoption) and would enable in-law traversal without two-hop path through SPOUSE_OF + PARENT_OF.

evidence: Soon after Lord Manfred Hightower died, and Addam succeeded his father as the Lord of the High Tower.
description: Node manfred-hightower-aegons-conquest has type: event.war in its frontmatter but is clearly a character (Manfred Hightower, Lord of the High Tower during Aegon's Conquest era). The parenthetical '(Aegon's Conquest)' in the node name apparently triggered an event.war classification during ingestion. This node is the father of Addam Hightower (addam-hightower node) and should have type: character.human. Node file exists at graph/nodes/characters/manfred-hightower-aegons-conquest.node.md. A SUCCEEDS edge was emitted from addam-hightower to this node despite the type mismatch (SUCCEEDS has no target type constraint and the prose unambiguously establishes the successor relationship). Recommend correcting the type field to character.human.

evidence: During the Dance of the Dragons, her father married again to Samantha Tarly, but Ormund died in the First Battle of Tumbleton.
description: The vocabulary lacks edge types for step-family relationships and in-law relationships (good-sister / good-brother in Westerosi usage). In Bethany Hightower's case: (1) Samantha Tarly married Bethany's father Ormund, making Samantha her step-mother. (2) After Ormund died, Samantha married Bethany's brother Lyonel, making Samantha her good-sister (sister-in-law). These kinship relationships are culturally significant in Westerosi society and appear frequently in ASOIAF. Proposed additions: STEP_PARENT_OF (step-parent → step-child), SIBLING_IN_LAW_OF or GOOD_SIBLING_OF (symmetric, for in-law sibling relationships). Example sentences: (1) 'During the Dance of the Dragons, her father married again to Samantha Tarly, but Ormund died in the First Battle of Tumbleton.' (2) 'Years later, Bethany's step-mother would become her good-sister when Sam married again to Ormund's heir, Lyonel.' (3) 'According to Samantha Tarly, Bethany was a beautiful girl with smooth soft skin, lustrous hair, and the sweetest manner.'

evidence: Later that same year, Lord Donnel died from the Shivers.
description: ASOIAF prose frequently names a specific disease or ailment as the cause of a character death. Examples: (1) donnel-hightower.node.md ## Origins: "Lord Donnel died from the Shivers" (target: concept.medical); (2) Many characters during the Great Spring Sickness and Pale Mare epidemics are described as dying from those diseases; (3) Greyscale kills characters explicitly named as dying of it. The existing KILLED_BY type expects a person/creature killer, not a disease agent. DIED_AT captures place of death, not cause. A DIED_OF(person → disease) edge would enable queries like "who died from Greyscale" or "which characters perished in the Great Spring Sickness." Proposed directionality: Character → Disease/Ailment (concept.medical).

evidence: Lord Triston raised the Starry Sept in Robeson's honor after his passing.
description: Two candidates in this batch involve a character building or commissioning a physical structure. (1) triston-hightower -> starry-sept: 'Lord Triston raised the Starry Sept in Robeson's honor.' Triston commissioned/built the Starry Sept. (2) robeson -> starry-sept: Robeson was the honoree of the Starry Sept (built in his honor after his death). No current vocab type covers patron-of-construction for place.location targets. FOUNDED covers organizations/houses/institutions but not buildings/places. OWNS captures static possession, not the act of commissioning construction. Proposed: COMMISSIONED (character -> place.location; person caused a structure to be built). Additional examples across the broader corpus: Aegon commissioned Dragonstone; Jaehaerys built the kingsroad; Harren the Black raised Harrenhal. A second related gap (q-002) proposes MEMORIALIZED_IN for the honoree direction.

evidence: Lord Triston raised the Starry Sept in Robeson's honor after his passing.
description: When a location is built or named in honor of a person, there is no canonical edge type to capture the relationship from the honoree to the commemorating place. Example: robeson -> starry-sept ('Lord Triston raised the Starry Sept in Robeson's honor'). BORN_AT/DIED_AT/BURIED_AT do not apply; the prose does not say Robeson was buried there. Additional examples in ASOIAF: Harren named Harrenhal after himself (Harren MEMORIALIZED_IN Harrenhal); the Red Keep's Tower of the Hand is named for the office it houses; Casterly Rock is named for Casterly who built it. Proposed: MEMORIALIZED_IN (person -> place.location; the place was erected, dedicated, or named in their honor). Pair with COMMISSIONED (q-001) which covers the builder direction.

evidence: She was a reputed witch
description: Character has a social reputation for being a magic practitioner or witch. No existing type covers attribution of a trait/reputation to a character. PERCEIVED_AS requires both endpoints to be characters. HAS_ATTRIBUTE would fit but does not exist. Proposed: REPUTED_AS(character -> concept/trait) for socially-attributed reputation edges.

evidence: Eon is one of Lysa Arryn's suitors after the death of Lord Jon Arryn. As such, he sends wine from his own cellars as a gift to Lysa.
description: Propose adding COURTS (Suitor → Courted) to capture romantic/marital courtship — a recurring ASOIAF pattern distinct from BETROTHED_TO (formal engagement), SPOUSE_OF (marriage), and LOVER_OF (sexual relationship). Examples: (1) 'Eon is one of Lysa Arryn's suitors after the death of Lord Jon Arryn' (eon-hunter courts lysa-arryn); (2) After Jon Arryn's death, multiple Vale lords including Nestor Royce and Eon Hunter court Lysa for political marriage; (3) In the Targaryen era, various lords court eligible noblewomen as political instruments. COURTS captures the active-pursuit phase before betrothal. Without it, suitor relationships must be either rejected (losing information) or forced into SEEKS (too broad) or NEGOTIATES_WITH (too diplomatic).

evidence: A pair of Renly's men, Ser Owen Inchfield and Ser Raymun Fossoway, had told her that if she wanted to enter a melee, she had to be willing to be treated as a man, then attempted to have their way with her. Ser Randyll Tarly stopped them.
description: No canonical edge type covers one character physically attacking or attempting to assault another. ASSAULTS is the obvious proposed type. Should we add ASSAULTS(person->person) to the locked vocabulary? Alternatively ATTACKS? The incident with Owen/Brienne is the clearest example but this pattern will recur.

evidence: Rickard Karstark was named after Lord Rickard Stark
description: No edge type exists for naming/eponym relationships. NAMED_AFTER or NAMESAKE_OF would cover cases where a person is named in honor of another person.

evidence: He is good friends with Ser Edmure Tully, the heir to Riverrun.
description: The prose-edge-classifier repeatedly encounters explicit friendship/companionship relationships between characters that do not fit any existing vocabulary type. ALLIES_WITH is for political alliances; TRUSTS is one-directional confidence; no type captures mutual personal friendship or companionship. Example sentences: (1) "He is good friends with Ser Edmure Tully, the heir to Riverrun." (patrek-mallister) (2) "Patrek Mallister was not too ill a fellow; they shared a taste for wenches, wine, and hawking." (Theon on Patrek) (3) "Ser Edmure Tully spends much of his time with his friends, Ser Marq Piper and Patrek." Proposed type COMPANION_OF would be symmetric and capture explicit narrative friendship/camaraderie. Without it, these candidates are rejected as just_mention even though they represent meaningful relational information for graph traversal.

evidence: During the Hour of the Wolf after the war, Medrick agreed to transport those men taking the black
description: Propose PARTICIPATES_IN for non-combat administrative/logistical involvement in a named event or political period. FIGHTS_IN requires combat participation, but many characters have meaningful involvement in named events (eras, political crises, councils) that is not combat. Example 1: Medrick agreed to transport prisoners during the Hour of the Wolf after the Dance of the Dragons — logistics, not battle. Example 2 (pattern): characters serving as jurors, witnesses, or administrators during a named event participate meaningfully but do not fight. Example 3 (pattern): maesters, septons, and smallfolk present at battles, sieges, or political events as non-combatants. The gap is: a character can FIGHTS_IN a battle or ATTENDS a ceremony but has no edge type for administrative participation in a named political period or crisis. PARTICIPATES_IN would cover person → event (any event.* type) for non-combat, non-ceremonial-attendance participation.

evidence: Lord Desmond and the childless Medrick died of Winter Fever four days apart in 132 AC
description: Propose DIED_OF for cause of death by disease, ailment, or natural condition — distinct from KILLED_BY (a person killer), EXECUTES (judicial), POISONS (poison method), and SACRIFICES (ritual). ASOIAF has many named diseases and conditions (Winter Fever, the Shivers, the Spring Sickness, greyscale, grey plague, the sweating sickness) that kill characters. Example 1: Lord Desmond and Medrick Manderly died of Winter Fever four days apart in 132 AC. Example 2 (pattern): Grand Maester Pycelle died — but by KILLS. The pattern of interest is disease-caused death, where a named pathogen or medical condition is the proximate cause. Example 3 (pattern): characters who die of old age, starvation, or named illness in the historical record appear frequently in Fire and Blood material. The gap: no canonical edge type captures person → concept.medical (or concept.disease) for the cause-of-death relationship. DIED_AT handles location; KILLED_BY handles persons; there is nothing for disease/condition causes. Proposed direction: character → disease/condition (concept.medical).

evidence: Lord Albin perished during the harsh winter of 59 AC from the Shivers.
description: The current vocabulary has no edge type for a character contracting or dying from a disease. Proposed: AFFLICTED_BY (character → concept.medical), covering both fatal and non-fatal affliction. This pattern recurs throughout ASOIAF prose. Example 1: 'Lord Albin perished during the harsh winter of 59 AC from the Shivers.' Example 2: Jorah Mormont contracts greyscale on the Sorrows. Example 3: Shireen Baratheon scarred by greyscale in childhood. KILLED_BY does not apply (disease is not a person-killer); DIED_AT captures location not cause. Without AFFLICTED_BY, the disease-character relationship is silently dropped.

evidence: Lord Lucifer was present at Visenya's Hill when King Maegor was challenged by Ser Damon Morrigen, Grand Captain of the Warrior's Sons, to a trial of seven.
description: trial-of-seven is classified as concept.custom in graph/nodes/concepts/trial-of-seven.node.md, but it represents a specific combat event (the Trial of Seven at Visenya's Hill, 42 AC). The FIGHTS_IN edge type requires an event.* target (per type-contract enforcement), so lucifer-massey's participation in the trial cannot be encoded as FIGHTS_IN. The trial-of-seven node may need to be reclassified as event.battle or event.tournament for combat-participation edges to be emittable. Affected candidate: lucifer-massey → trial-of-seven (characters-house-massey bucket). Currently rejected with reason: type-contract-violation-trial-of-seven-is-concept.custom-not-event.*-fights_in-requires-event-target.

Need edge type for non-lethal physical attack/assault. Mandon Moore slashes Tyrion across the nose and beats Sansa on Joffrey orders. ATTACKS_ON is not in vocab; FIGHTS_IN requires event target; KILLS/WOUNDED_BY do not fit (no death, attacker-known). Propose ASSAULTS(character->character) or WOUNDS(character->character, qualifier=non-lethal). Currently these are rejected with reason vocab-gap-no-non-lethal-assault-type.
